text: Lufthansa provides specialized assistance services for passengers with various types of impairments, including sensory impairments (hearing, visual, and cognitive) and reduced mobility. Passengers can register their specific assistance requirements online.

In the event of a damaged or lost mobility aid, Lufthansa provides the following options: 1) Lufthansa will cover the costs of repairing or replacing the device with one of comparable functionality and safety, or 2) the passenger may arrange for repair or replacement through a provider of their choice, in which case Lufthansa will manage the transport and pay the provider directly for the costs incurred.

section: Services for Passengers with Cognitive Impairment
text: To assist passengers with cognitive impairments in navigating airports, Lufthansa offers accompaniment throughout the airport, priority boarding and disembarking, and special instructions during delays or other travel-related problems. Passengers can register for this support online. While a traveling companion may be helpful, passengers must follow all crew instructions during the flight for their own safety.
Passengers who wish to travel with a companion can register them through the Lufthansa Service Centre.

Lufthansa participates in the Hidden Disabilities Sunflower programme to support passengers with invisible disabilities. Wearing the sunflower symbol is a discreet way to signal that a passenger may need additional time or assistance. While the symbol does not guarantee specific special services, staff will make every effort to ensure the passenger travels in comfort.

Lufthansa provides tailored assistance and information for various types of impairments, including sensory impairments (hearing and visual), cognitive impairments, and reduced mobility, as well as specific information for travel to the USA.
Passengers requiring help at the airport can register for assistance online. Lufthansa provides accompaniment throughout the airport experience for those who have registered for support.
For the safety of all passengers, the instructions provided by the crew during the flight must be followed at all times.
A travelling companion may be helpful for passengers requiring extra support. Companions can be registered via the Service Centre.

Lufthansa offers specialized assistance requirements for the following categories: sensory impairment (hearing and visual), cognitive impairment, and reduced mobility, including specific guidelines for travel to the USA.
Lufthansa's website provides general navigation options for managing flights, viewing offers and destinations, handling baggage, check-in, and accessing airport travel briefings.
Customer support and account management services include Travel ID, Miles & More, the Lufthansa app, feedback mechanisms, and newsletter subscriptions.

Corporate resources include company information, career opportunities, and specialized services for travel agents and business clients, such as the Lufthansa Group for Business and the Corporate bonus programme. Note that some corporate links lead to external websites which may have different privacy, security, or accessibility policies.

Lufthansa reserves the right to refuse carriage or transport an assistance dog in the cargo hold for a surcharge if the dog does not behave appropriately. The owner is liable for any damage and additional costs resulting from the carriage of the dog.

Assistance dogs can be registered through the following channels: online, via the Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre, the Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre in the USA, the Help Centre, or through a travel agency.

General advice for the carriage of animals in aircraft applies to assistance dogs in the cabin. Lufthansa distinguishes between assistance dogs on flights outside the United States and those on non-stop flights to/from the United States. Passengers must ensure that carrying the dog as additional carry-on baggage complies with the entry and exit regulations of all countries involved in the journey.
An assistance dog, regardless of breed or type, is specially trained to perform tasks or work for a person with an impairment. Necessary veterinary examinations or treatments may be required.

Passengers may register their assistance dogs online, by emailing the Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre (including the USA center), via the Help Centre, or through a travel agency. General advice for the carriage of animals in aircraft also applies to these dogs.
Lufthansa provides services for travellers with reduced mobility or sensory or cognitive impairments to ensure they feel safe, cared for, and welcome at all times.

For questions or further information, the Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre (MOC) can be contacted via Fax: +49 - 69 69 - 68 36 77 or Email: specialservice@dlh.de. Operating hours are Monday to Friday (08:30-20:00) and Saturday, Sunday, and public holidays (08:30-16:30).
Travellers to and from the USA can contact the Medical Desk in the USA at Tel: +1-516-279-1768. Operating hours are Monday to Friday (09:00 to 17:00).
If booking a ticket by telephone, please inform Lufthansa of your individual assistance needs before completing the booking.
Lufthansa provides an online service for requesting assistance, available in German and English. A completed online booking is required to use this service.
When booking through a travel agency, please inform the agency of your individual assistance needs before completing the booking to ensure the best possible service.
To ensure optimal preparation, assistance dogs should ideally be registered for transport in the cabin no later than 48 hours before departure.

A training certificate is required for assistance dogs. This certificate must be issued by a recognized training facility, a qualified dog trainer, or an institution accredited by one of the following organizations: Messerli Research Institute, Assistance Dogs International (ADI), Assistance Dogs Europe (ADEu), or the International Guide Dog Federation (IGDF).

section: Assistance Dog Identification and US Travel Rules
text: Assistance dogs must wear a clearly visible identification tag. For travel outside the United States, a completed DOT Form or a certificate of completed obedience training is not sufficient documentation.

Lufthansa provides various options for the carriage of animals, including assistance dogs, animals as excess baggage, and animals as additional carry-on baggage. Specific regulations apply to fighting dog breeds and snub-nosed animals.
Lufthansa provides special services before, during, and after flights for passengers with cognitive disabilities or sensory impairments (hearing or visual).
Passengers can register their assistance requirements via telephone during the booking process, through a travel agency, or online after the booking has been completed.
To ensure the best possible support, passengers should notify Lufthansa of their assistance requirements at least 48 hours before departure.

The Lufthansa Medical Operation Centre (MOC) provides assistance via Fax at +49 - 69 69 - 68 36 77 and Email at specialservice@dlh.de. Operating hours are Monday to Friday from 08:30 to 20:00, and Saturday, Sunday, and public holidays from 08:30 to 16:30.
Travellers to and from the USA can contact the Medical Desk in the USA at Tel: +1-516-279-1768. Operating hours are Monday to Friday from 09:00 to 17:00.
For the safety and well-being of the passenger during their trip, it may sometimes be necessary to travel with a companion.

A companion is required if a passenger: cannot understand safety instructions from the cabin crew, is unable to bring themselves to safety in the event of an emergency, or requires personal assistance on board or in the lounge.

Passengers with reduced mobility are carried in accordance with EU Regulation No. 1107/2006. Passengers travelling to or from the USA are carried in accordance with U.S. DOT provisions, Title 14, Code of Federal Regulations, Part 382.

Complaint Resolution Officers (CRO) are available during opening hours at all USA destinations, as well as in Frankfurt and Munich, to assist passengers directly with any problems. Crew members or ground staff can facilitate contact with a CRO.

Lufthansa offers various specialized services for passengers, including accessible travel, healthy journey options, services for older travellers, Global Healthcare, Doctor on Board, and medical travel companions.
Lufthansa participates in the Hidden Disabilities Sunflower programme. Wearing the sunflower symbol is a discreet way for passengers to indicate that they have an invisible disability.
Lufthansa provides specific support and helpful tips to ensure that travel for passengers with visual impairments is as safe, easy, and comfortable as possible.
Passengers can register their support requirements online through the Service Centre. Specific information is also available for registering assistance dogs.
Lufthansa offers accompaniment throughout the airport, assistance with boarding and disembarking at the gate, and separate briefings regarding delays or important travel announcements.
Cabin crew provide personalized safety instructions, orientation assistance within the cabin, and detailed explanations of the meal arrangement on the tray.
At the destination airport, staff are available to assist passengers with exiting the airport or navigating to connecting flights.
Support requirements and assistance dog registrations can be managed online via the Service Centre.
Lufthansa provides accompaniment for passengers throughout the airport and offers check-in information to support the travel process.
Cabin crew provide separate, personal safety instructions to passengers on board.
Lufthansa staff are available to provide assistance to passengers at their destination airport or if they need to change flights.
The Be My Eyes app is recommended as a valuable tool to provide assistance for people with impaired vision, making travel easier.
Lufthansa recognizes that flying with a hearing impairment can present particular challenges and offers specific services to assist these passengers.
Passengers can register their specific assistance requirements online through the Lufthansa booking and reservation system.
Lufthansa provides specific information and registration procedures for the carriage of assistance dogs.

Lufthansa offers accompaniment throughout the airport upon request. This includes assistance at the gate during boarding and disembarking (preferably before other passengers) and the provision of separate instructions in the event of travel delays.

On-board, cabin crew provide personalized safety instructions to passengers with accessibility needs. Additionally, a wide range of films with subtitles in German and English are available for passengers.

Lufthansa provides specialized assistance and information for passengers with various needs, including reduced mobility, sensory impairments (hearing, visual, and cognitive impairments), and specific requirements for travel to the USA.
Passengers can register their assistance requirements online through the provided registration link.
Detailed information regarding the registration and carriage of assistance dogs is available via the dedicated information link.
Lufthansa offers accompaniment for passengers throughout their entire time at the airport if required.
Passengers may receive separate instructions and accompaniment throughout their time at the airport, including during the check-in process.
Cabin crew will provide separate, personal safety instructions to passengers who require them while on board.

Lufthansa provides specialized accessibility services for passengers with reduced mobility, those with sensory impairments (including hearing, visual, and cognitive impairments), and specific travel information for the USA.
An assistance dog, regardless of breed or type, is specially trained to perform tasks for individuals with visual, sensory, psychiatric, intellectual, or other cognitive impairments.
Assistance dogs are permitted on any route free of charge, provided that specific requirements for cabin entry are met.

To ensure safe and undisturbed flight operations, assistance dogs must obey commands and behave appropriately in public spaces (no barking, growling, or jumping). Additionally, dogs must not defecate in an uncontrolled manner in the cabin or at the gate to maintain hygiene and health standards.
When following links to external websites, please be aware that these sites may not adhere to the same privacy, security, or accessibility policies as Lufthansa.

Lufthansa uses necessary cookies to operate its app and website. With user consent, additional cookies are utilized: Analytics cookies to evaluate performance and campaign efficiency; Personalisation cookies to provide a customized experience and usage-based offers; and Advertising cookies from third-party companies to deliver targeted ads on social media and the internet.

Users may give, refuse, or withdraw consent for cookie usage at any time via the link provided at the bottom of each page. Further details on data collection and sharing are available in the Privacy Policy and Cookie Policy.
The website provides navigation options for booking and preparation, discovering Lufthansa, and accessing assistance requirements and services for passengers with reduced mobility.

WCHR assistance is provided to the exit at the gate. This service is for passengers who can move independently on board the aircraft and use stairs, but require a wheelchair or other means of transport for long distances within the airport.

WCHS assistance is provided to the aircraft. This service is for passengers who can move independently on board the aircraft but cannot walk up or down stairs and require a wheelchair or other means of transport to navigate the airport.

WCHC assistance is for passengers who are unable to walk and can only travel with their own wheelchair. These passengers require assistance from airport arrival, through all phases of boarding and disembarking (including onboard the aircraft), until arrival at their destination.
Passengers who wish to bring their own wheelchair will be provided with a wheelchair free of charge at the airport.
Passengers can transport their own foldable wheelchairs in the cargo hold free of charge.
Passengers requiring wheelchair assistance at their departure or destination airport must inform the airline in a timely manner, either during the booking process or afterwards.
Passengers may take up to two mobility aids. When bringing a personal wheelchair, the passenger must specify its dimensions, weight, and any special features.
Smaller aircraft may have size restrictions. A list of aircraft door dimensions by aircraft type is available for reference.
At most stations, foldable wheelchairs can be used until the passenger reaches the gate. At check-in, these wheelchairs receive a 'Delivery at aircraft' tag and are loaded accordingly.

WCHR: Wheelchair assistance to the exit at the gate. This service is for passengers who can move independently on board the aircraft and use stairs, but require a wheelchair or other transport for long distances within the airport.

WCHR: Wheelchair assistance to the exit at the gate. This is intended for passengers who can move independently on board the aircraft and navigate stairs, but need assistance for long distances within the airport.
WCHS: Wheelchair assistance to the aircraft.

WCHS: Wheelchair assistance to the aircraft. This service is for passengers who can move independently on board the aircraft but cannot walk up or down stairs and require a wheelchair to get around the airport.

WCHC: Wheelchair assistance for passengers unable to walk. This service provides comprehensive assistance from airport arrival, through all boarding and disembarking phases (including on board the aircraft), until arrival at the final destination. Passengers in this category typically travel with their own wheelchair.

Battery-powered wheelchairs may be subject to safety-related restrictions and specific regulations. Passengers must complete a designated form and send it to specialcases@dlh.de to verify if transport is possible.
Lufthansa provides specialized services for passengers with reduced mobility or sensory or cognitive impairments to ensure they feel safe, cared for, and welcome throughout their journey.
To ensure individual needs are met, passengers must notify Lufthansa of their assistance requirements no later than 48 hours before departure.
Groups traveling with their own mobility aids are requested to notify Lufthansa of their specific care requirements as early as possible.

While Lufthansa strives to provide service for short-notice registrations, it cannot be guaranteed. Without advance registration, neither the requested assistance nor travel on the booked flight can be guaranteed.
Many airports provide their own assistance services for passengers. Travelers should check directly with the airports on their itinerary for specific details.
Passengers can report their individual assistance needs by calling 0800 8384267. This is recommended when booking via telephone or through a travel agency before the booking is completed.
Lufthansa's online service for reporting assistance needs is available in English and German.
Passengers may request assistance via an online form, provided that the online booking has already been completed.
When booking through a travel agency, passengers should inform the agency of their individual assistance needs before completing the booking to ensure the best possible service.

Passengers can register their assistance requirements online or by calling 0800 - 83 84 267. It is recommended to register in advance, as Lufthansa cannot guarantee the best support for short-notice registrations.

Passengers are generally free to choose the seat that best meets their requirements, although some safety restrictions may apply. Most seats on the Lufthansa fleet feature moveable armrests to facilitate easier access.
If a passenger wishes to use a seating device, the device must be checked by the Medical Operation Centre beforehand for safety reasons.
When booking a trip, passengers can specify the services they need on the ground and during the flight to help Lufthansa provide the necessary support.

Visually impaired passengers do not require a fitness for air travel assessment. Available assistance includes separate briefings for delays and safety, airport escorts, explanations of meal tray arrangements, and access to waiting lounges during longer stopovers in Frankfurt or Munich.

Hearing-impaired passengers are provided with separate briefings for delays and safety, as well as films with German and English subtitles. Staff are available to assist at destinations or during flight changes.
For hearing-impaired passengers with longer stopovers in Frankfurt or Munich, Lufthansa provides access to a dedicated waiting lounge.

Lufthansa carries passengers with cognitive impairments, provided they are able to follow the crew's safety instructions during the flight (e.g., during turbulence). It is noted that international airports can be stressful due to long routes and security/customs checkpoints, and passengers with conditions causing disorientation (e.g., Alzheimer's) may face risks regarding their ability to pay attention to safety instructions.
Certain requirements must be met to take an assistance dog into the cabin. Detailed information regarding the carriage of assistance dogs is available on a dedicated page.
Passengers are generally free to choose seats based on their individual needs. However, for safety reasons, restrictions apply to all seats in the emergency exit rows regardless of aircraft type.
On B747-400 and B747-800 aircraft, seats on the upper deck are not suitable for passengers categorized as WCHS or WCHC, as the upper deck is only accessible via a staircase.

In the A350-900 Allegris cabin, the following seats are unsuitable for specific mobility needs: 
- First Class: 1K is not suitable for WCHC.
- Business Class: Suites 2A-K and 8A-K, Privacy Seats 3A+K, 5A+K, 7A+K, 9A+K, 11A+K, and Extra Space Seats 3E, 5F, 9E, 11F are not suitable for WCHC.
- Economy Class: Rows 19-K, 26A-C, and 26H-K are not suitable for WCHC, WCHS, and WCHR.

Most seats across the Lufthansa fleet feature movable armrests to facilitate easier access. If a passenger wishes to attach a seating device to their seat, the device must be checked by Lufthansa beforehand for safety reasons.
Passengers can register battery-powered wheelchairs by contacting the Medical Operation Centre.

section: Reporting Damaged or Delayed Mobility Aids
text: If a mobility aid (wheelchair or electric mobility scooter) is damaged or delayed, passengers should report the issue immediately, either online or at the destination airport's lost-property office.
When a mobility aid is delayed, passengers may choose to either collect the device at the destination airport or have it delivered to a suitable location, such as their home or hotel.
Lufthansa will reimburse transport costs to or from the airport incurred due to a delayed mobility aid, provided that proof of expenses is submitted.

Passengers whose mobility aids are damaged during a flight are entitled to a loan wheelchair that meets their functional and safety requirements until their own device is returned, repaired, or replaced. If Lufthansa cannot provide a suitable loan device, passengers may organize one themselves and will be reimbursed for the costs within 30 days of submitting proof.

section: Repair or Replacement of Mobility Aids
text: In the event of a lost or damaged mobility aid, Lufthansa will cover the costs of repairing the device or provide a replacement with comparable functionality and safety.

A travel companion is required if the passenger cannot help themselves in the event of an evacuation, cannot communicate with flight attendants regarding safety instructions, or cannot understand or follow the safety demonstration.
Lufthansa staff cannot provide the following services: assistance in the toilet, lifting or carrying passengers, assisting passengers with meals, or administering/supervising the taking of medicines.
Information is available regarding the requirements and role of travel companions for passengers with accessibility needs.

Further information is available regarding accessible travel, services for passengers with reduced mobility, specific information for the USA, and support for passengers with sensory impairments (hearing, visual, and cognitive).

Lufthansa provides information and registration for passengers with specific accessibility needs, including those with reduced mobility and sensory impairments (hearing, visual, and cognitive impairments), as well as specific assistance requirements for travel to the USA.
The website offers navigation for general travel management, including viewing and amending flights, baggage information, check-in, airport services, travel briefings, and destination guides.
Users can access customer services, manage their Travel ID and Miles & More accounts, use the Lufthansa app, and provide feedback or sign up for the newsletter.
The site provides corporate information through 'About us' and 'Jobs & careers' sections.
Lufthansa provides links to external websites regarding partners and sustainability. These external sites may not adhere to the same privacy, security, or accessibility policies as Lufthansa.
